factors.name: US Inflation (CPI)
factors.summary: US CPI printed 3.8% YoY in April vs 3.7% expected, with energy accounting for 40% of the increase. Hot inflation lifts Fed rate-hike odds to ~30% for December, directly pressuring real yields lower and gold negatively.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations / Breakevens
factors.summary: 5-year breakeven rates hit multiyear highs (highest since Oct 2022), 10-year at 2.5% (peak since 2023). Rising inflation expectations typically compress real yields and support nominal yields, creating near-term headwinds for gold despite lower real yields.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: Not explicitly stated in news, but implied: higher nominal yields + higher breakevens = real yields likely stable or slightly lower. Mixed signal; needs confirmation on actual TIPS levels.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY holding steady ~98.30 after two days of gains; Goldman Sachs predicts broad dollar strength as energy shock keeps yields elevated. Stronger USD is structurally bearish for gold.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy Outlook
factors.summary: Hot inflation and energy shock raising market expectations for Fed rate hike; Morgan Stanley maps scenarios including 100bp hike risk. Hawkish tilt weighs on gold despite energy-driven inflation.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran / Strait of Hormuz)
factors.summary: Iran has restored access to 30 of 33 missile sites; Hormuz closure ongoing with vessel traffic at ~5% of pre-war levels. Persistent Middle East tension typically supports safe-haven demand for gold, though Trump downplaying Iran focus in Xi summit.

factors.name: Oil Prices (WTI/Brent)
factors.summary: Oil dipped after 3-day 8% rally; Hormuz closure and Iran tensions keep price floor elevated. Rising oil → higher inflation → lower real yields (bullish gold) but also higher nominal yields (bearish gold). Currently mixed.

factors.name: US Treasury Yields (nominal 10y)
factors.summary: Yields rising post-hot CPI; Japanese 20-year yields at 1997 highs; broader global yield curve steepening. Higher nominal yields are directly bearish for gold (lower opportunity cost of holding USD).

factors.name: Central Bank Policy: BoJ / Japan
factors.summary: OECD projects BoJ hiking to 2% by end-2027 (from 0.75% now). BOJ hawkish tone supports yen carry unwind, strengthening USD/JPY, which is bearish for gold via stronger dollar.

factors.name: BoJ Hawkish Pivot
factors.summary: USD/JPY at ~157.70, up three consecutive days as markets price BoJ tightening. Yen carry trades unwinding = weaker Asian safe-haven flows + stronger USD headwind for XAUUSD.

factors.name: India Gold Tariff Hike (15% from 6%)
factors.summary: India more than doubled gold/silver import tariffs in bid to defend rupee and FX reserves. Near-term demand suppression risk for largest physical gold consumer; structural headwind for gold price.

factors.name: Trade / China Risk
factors.summary: Trump heading to China for Xi summit; Nvidia CEO Huang joining delegation. Focus on trade talks rather than Iran de-escalation; outcome uncertain. Could shift geopolitical risk premium lower if deal signals optimism.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment / Equity Action
factors.summary: S&P 500 pared losses despite hot CPI; Korean retail buyers stepped in; mixed risk sentiment. Not a panic/safe-haven flush; equities not decisively down, limiting immediate gold safe-haven bid.

factors.name: Global Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No recent news on PBoC, RBI, or EM central bank gold purchases in this window. Structural bid likely present but not visible in today's flow data.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows (GLD/IAU)
factors.summary: No ETF flow data mentioned in news. Institutional positioning unknown; unable to assess.

screening.narrative: Gold is caught in a crossfire: hot CPI (3.8% vs 3.7% exp) and rising breakeven inflation expectations are lifting nominal Treasury yields and the USD, both bearish for gold in the short term. However, persistent Middle East tensions (Iran missile site rebuilds, Hormuz closure) and geopolitical uncertainty around Trump-Xi talks provide underlying safe-haven bid. The BoJ's hawkish pivot and India's tariff hike on gold imports add near-term headwinds. Currently, the inflation-driven real yield compression is being overwhelmed by nominal yield rises and dollar strength, creating a NEUTRAL near-term setup with a slight bear lean.
catalysts_ahead: US Producer Price Index (PPI) April – Wednesday May 13, due later in Asian session
Trump-Xi summit in China this week – could shift geopolitical risk premium
FOMC decision / Powell remarks – market pricing ~30% probability of December hike
Any Iran de-escalation signal – would reduce safe-haven bid
Further BoJ policy clarity – could reinforce USD/JPY strength if hawkish
RBI / Indian monetary policy signal on gold demand suppression
Hormuz strait shipping data / Iranian compliance updates
screening.risk_warning: If Trump-Xi summit yields trade deal/de-escalation signal, geopolitical premium could evaporate and gold could break below 4,680. Conversely, if Hormuz tensions escalate sharply or oil price spikes above $140+, safe-haven bid and inflation narrative could flip gold decisively higher. Real yields (TIPS) falling unexpectedly would also be bullish for gold despite higher nominal yields. Monitor Fed rate-hike odds closely; any hawkish surprise from Powell or core inflation re-acceleration could slam gold lower. India's tariff hike is a wild card for physical demand; if it sparks smuggling or demand destruction beyond consensus, could pressure prices further.
